acceptance_criteria: After saving a rich document via the native editor, at least one FlightEvent with action='document_saved' and schema='hsk.native_editor@0.1' appears in GET /api/flight_recorder filtered by actor_id='native_editor_human' — proven by integration test against real PG.
After a canvas node placement, at least one FlightEvent with action='canvas_node_placed' appears in the flight recorder — proven by integration test.
After an undo_fired event, at least one FlightEvent with action='undo_fired' appears in the flight recorder — proven by integration test.
emit() failures (e.g. backend unreachable) do not crash the egui frame or block the UI; the error is silently logged to the in-memory error ring — proven by unit test simulating a failed POST.
surface_extension_seam.rs compiles with cargo check (trait stubs are valid Rust, no unused-import errors beyond the allowed dead_code suppress) — proven by cargo check in CI.

EditorSurfaceRegistry with a registered mock surface receives on_selection_changed and on_event_emitted callbacks during a SharedSelection change and a document_saved emit respectively — proven by unit test.
FlightRecorderPane widget loads and displays at least one native editor event when one exists in the backend — proven by kittest after an integration-test event is emitted.
cargo test -p handshake_native_frontend event_emitter -- --nocapture passes with no failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_native_frontend event_emitter -- --nocapture  =>  all PASS
cargo check -p handshake_native_frontend  =>  surface_extension_seam.rs compiles without errors
integration test (real PG): save rich doc, GET /api/flight_recorder?actor=native_editor_human, assert 'document_saved' event present
integration test: canvas node place, GET flight recorder, assert 'canvas_node_placed' event present
unit test: failed POST in emit() => error added to error ring, no panic, no egui frame block
kittest: open FlightRecorderPane, assert at least one 'fr-event-*' ListItem present after document save
implementation_notes: POST /api/flight_recorder/runtime_chat_event is the correct endpoint — confirmed at api.ts line ~2339 (the React emitRuntimeChatEvent function). Body must be RuntimeChatEventV0_1 (api.ts line ~607): include schema_version='hsk.fr.runtime_chat@0.1', event_id (UUID), ts_utc (RFC3339), session_id, wsid. The native_editor_event payload goes in a nested JSON field.
tokio::spawn for emit: the app must have a tokio runtime. If the native app uses a different async executor, adapt accordingly. Use a oneshot channel to log the result back to the error ring.

The EditorSurface trait uses object safety rules: all methods take &self (not self), use Box<dyn EditorSurface>. Avoid generic methods in the trait; use concrete types (SharedSelection, NativeEditorEvent) to preserve object safety.

FlightRecorderView.tsx (React reference): imports getEvents (api.ts line 2316) with FlightEventFilters. The native FlightRecorderPane uses the same endpoint. Filter eventType='system' and use actor filter to isolate native editor events.

React reference for event types: FlightEvent.event_type (api.ts line 574) shows 'system' is the appropriate type for native editor events — do not invent 'native_editor' as a new event_type; the backend schema is closed.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/FlightRecorderView.tsx — getEvents, FlightEventFilters, event_type options, filter UI pattern, createDiagnostic for nav failures
app/src/lib/api.ts lines 568-600 — FlightEvent schema, event_type enum, actor field
app/src/lib/api.ts lines 607-617 — RuntimeChatEventV0_1 schema (the POST body format for flight recorder events)
binds_backend_api: POST /api/flight_recorder/runtime_chat_event  =>  api.ts line ~2339 emitRuntimeChatEvent  =>  body: RuntimeChatEventV0_1 { schema_version, event_id, ts_utc, session_id, wsid, ... }
GET /api/flight_recorder  =>  api.ts line 2316 getEvents  =>  params FlightEventFilters { actor, eventType, from, to, wsid }  =>  returns FlightEvent[]
POST /api/diagnostics  =>  api.ts line 841 createDiagnostic  =>  used to log emit failures as diagnostic events when the error ring is full

risks: RuntimeChatEventV0_1 body format mismatch: if the native emitter sends a field name that the backend rejects (e.g. camelCase vs snake_case), the event is silently dropped or the POST returns 422; the flight recorder appears empty even when the editor is active.
tokio::spawn for emit creates unbounded task accumulation if the backend is slow and the editor is very active (e.g. rapid code edits); without backpressure, the spawn queue grows unbounded.
EditorSurface trait object safety: adding a method with a generic parameter later breaks the dyn trait; the initial design must be carefully constrained to object-safe signatures.
surface_extension_seam.rs compiles today but may become stale as other modules evolve (type renames, moved imports); if allowed to drift it becomes misleading documentation.
Actor ID 'native_editor_human' conflicts with existing actor_id conventions in the backend if those are validated against a registry; events may be rejected.
minimum_controls: Write a unit test that serializes NativeEditorEventEmitter.build_post_body() and asserts every required RuntimeChatEventV0_1 field is present with correct snake_case keys; compare against the api.ts schema definition.

Add a semaphore (tokio::sync::Semaphore with permits=20) around spawn in emit(); if all permits are taken, drop the event and log to the in-memory error ring rather than spawning. This bounds concurrency.

Before finalizing the EditorSurface trait, write all expected future method signatures as comments and verify they are object-safe (no generics, no Self by value, no associated types without default); include this check in code review criteria.
Add a CI check: cargo check --all-targets (includes surface_extension_seam.rs); fail the build if it does not compile clean.

Use a descriptive but valid actor_id format: 'hsk:native_editor:{pane_id}' — check backend actor validation rules in src/backend/handshake_core/src before committing to a format; if a registry check exists, add the native editor actor to the allowed set via a backend seeding migration (raise as a typed backend blocker if needed).
